title: Extract shared SPSC cursor/publish core used by both rings
description:
The max code review (PR #2) flagged that RingBuffer (src/spsc.rs) and BytesRingBuffer (src/spsc_bytes.rs) duplicate ~150 lines of concurrency-critical code near-verbatim: Inner layout (padded cursor atomics + wait strategies), the NonNull handle-caching constructor block, the reload-once has_space/has_item checks and wait loops, flush(), the adaptive publish rule (caught-up / was-full / batch-boundary), and the Consumer Drop publish+notify.

Drift already happened once within PR #2 itself: the bytes ring shipped without the publish-batch clamp that the fixed ring had (review finding, since fixed).

Task: extract a crate-private cursor-core module (shared Inner, handle-cached pointer set, occupancy checks on wrapped cursor differences, wait loops, adaptive flush machinery) that both rings instantiate — the fixed ring at element granularity, the bytes ring at byte granularity with its own batch clamp (publish_batch vs publish_batch_bytes) and frame layer on top.

Constraints:
- No hot-path regression: re-run examples/bench (expect ~860-900 M msgs/s pinned spin strategies on Grace cores 4/5) and examples/bench_bytes (~4.9 ns per 8B msg) before/after.
- Preserve all review-fix semantics: wrap-safe comparisons, was-full immediate flush, Drop publish+notify, PopRef drop-guard panic safety, drain at-most-once.
- Keep the public API unchanged (RingBuffer/BytesRingBuffer, push/pop naming, WaitStrategy bounds on handles).
